# Code of Conduct

This project adopts the **Contributor Covenant, version 2.1**, in full and without modification.

- Canonical text: <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/>
- Translations: <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations/>
- FAQ: <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq/>

By participating in this project — opening issues, submitting pull requests, commenting in Discussions, or any other interaction — you agree to abide by the Contributor Covenant 2.1.

## Enforcement

Conduct concerns are handled by the project maintainer.

- **Preferred channel:** open a private report via GitHub's "Report a vulnerability" form at <https://github.com/pau-vega/nordvpn-action/security/advisories/new>. Mark the title with `[CoC]` so it routes correctly. The same private channel is used for security reports — see [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md). This is a single-maintainer project; the reporting channel is the same person.
- **Fallback:** if the GitHub form is unavailable, email the maintainer listed in the repo profile with the subject prefix `[CoC]`.

Reports are reviewed in private. The maintainer commits to:

- Acknowledging the report within 3 business days.
- Keeping the reporter's identity confidential unless they consent to disclosure.
- Following the enforcement ladder defined in the [Contributor Covenant 2.1 Enforcement Guidelines](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/#enforcement-guidelines) (correction → warning → temporary ban → permanent ban).

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to all project spaces — the GitHub repository (issues, PRs, Discussions, code review), the GitHub Marketplace listing if/when published, and any official channels the maintainer publishes in the future. It also applies when an individual is officially representing the project in public spaces.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct adopts the Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available at <https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/>. Contributor Covenant is released under the [Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/).
